revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積百足]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0672|7|29}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Hozumi Mushimaro|qid=Q22129398|1=Hozumi Mushimaro}}|father={{ill|Hozumi no Kujii|qid=Q22129343|1=Hozumi no Kujii}}|relatives={{ill|Hozumi no Ioe|qid=Q11597067|1=Hozumi no Ioe}}}}
'''Hozumi no Momotari''' (穂積 百足) was a figure from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} in Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=kabane|1=Kabane}} was {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}. He was the son of {{ill|Hozumi no Kusubi|ja|穂積咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Kusubi|1=Hozumi no Kujii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129343}}, and some genealogies suggest he had a son named {{ill|Hozumi no Mushimaro|ja|穂積虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Mushimaro|1=Hozumi Mushimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129398}}.<ref>Hozumi genealogy as cited in Ryō Ōta, ''Seishi Kakei Daijiten'' (Kadokawa Shoten, 1963).</ref>
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} of 672, he was killed while assembling troops for Prince Ōtomo (later {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|qid=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kōbun|1=Emperor Kōbun}}) in Yamato.
== Career == In June of 672, after learning of Prince Ōama’s (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}) rebellion, the court at {{ill|Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|en|Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|qid=Q11638487|lt=Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|1=Ōmi Ōtsu Palace}} dispatched emissaries to gather troops to suppress the uprising. Hozumi no Momotari, his younger brother {{ill|Hozumi no Ihoki|ja|穂積五百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Ihoki|1=Hozumi no Ioe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597067}}, and {{ill|Mononobe no Hinata|ja|物部日向|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Hinata|1=mononobe no himuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570695}} were sent to Yamato (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}), specifically to {{ill|Asuka|ja|飛鳥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka|qq=}}, the site of an old capital known as the Yamato Capital (倭京). There, they worked alongside the local governor {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11669026}} to organize troops. Their camp was located west of the {{ill|Asuka-dera|en|Asuka-dera|qid=Q779755|lt=Asukadera Temple|1=Asuka-dera}} under a zelkova tree.
At the same time, {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}} had secretly allied with Prince Ōama and was preparing to seize control of the region. Fukei collaborated with another local official, {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11424804}}, to devise a plan. Fukei would approach the camp disguised as {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|qid=Q837590|lt=Prince Takeda|1=Prince Takechi}}, spreading a false report that Prince Takeda’s forces had already arrived in Yamato. Kumake, who had inside knowledge of the camp, would help orchestrate an internal defection.
On June 29, Fukei entered the camp west of Asukadera Temple and successfully seized control with the help of Kumake’s internal defection. At that time, Momotari was stationed at the military arsenal in {{ill|Kokiden|ja|小墾田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, overseeing the transport of weapons to {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}. Fukei, claiming to act under Prince Takeda’s orders, summoned Momotari to the camp.
Momotari approached slowly on horseback and arrived at the zelkova tree near Asukadera Temple. When asked to dismount, he delayed, moving at a sluggish pace. An individual pulled him from his horse, and he was struck by an arrow and then killed with a sword. With this, Yamato's forces defected to Prince Ōama’s side, significantly bolstering his position in the Jinshin War.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}
